goal: 2026-06-05-session-sources: I4 - Align docs and release skill

summary.title: Align cxs source docs and release skill
summary.what: Updated current-state documentation and the distributable cxs skill source to describe the implemented source foundation: codex is the only public source, --source codex is optional/default, selectors and coverage are source-aware, and claude-code remains reserved/non-public.
summary.why: I4 needed the public docs and release skill source to match the already implemented I1-I3 behavior without implying that Claude Code support, npm release behavior, global cxs, or installed skills had been updated.
summary.user_goal: 2026-06-05-session-sources: I4 - Align docs and release skill

decisions.chose: Document only codex as public and describe claude-code as reserved/non-public.
decisions.rationale: The checkout rejects non-public sources with unsupported_source and has no public Claude Code adapter.
rejected: Advertising Claude Code source support before implementation and release
Leaving docs as generic multi-source wording that could be read as current support

decisions.chose: State that docs and skill changes are checkout/source-layer only until a separate release/install workflow updates npm, global cxs, or installed skills.
decisions.rationale: The task explicitly forbids publishing, global skill install, local PATH CLI update, or overclaiming installed behavior.

decisions.chose: Use docs whitespace checks, checkout CLI help, unsupported-source smoke, and full npm run check.
decisions.rationale: The change is docs/skill-only, but json-schema and skill text mirror code contracts, so the full check was cheap and useful.

rejected.alternative: Inspect or update installed global cxs/skill state
rejected.reason: I4 is a checkout/source update and the task explicitly forbids mutating installed tools.

review_notes: Actual I4 commit files are README.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/ROADMAP.md, skill-packages/cxs/** references, and the I4 handoff.
Mainline preflight reported same-workflow stacked overlaps with D1/I1/I2/I3/control-plane intents plus notes rewrite drift; this was classified as expected workflow overlap, not a semantic conflict.
No code, private dogfood data, npm release files, global skill state, installed CLI, push, PR, or Claude Code adapter was changed.
Validation: git diff --check, npm run cxs -- --help, npm run cxs -- status --help, npm run cxs -- status --source claude-code --json, npm run check.

architectural_claims: cxs has a source adapter boundary with codex as the only public source in this checkout.
selector, coverage, DB identity, and query/read paths are source-aware in the implemented foundation.
claude-code is reserved/non-public and not available as a public sync/query source.
behavioral_changes: No runtime behavior changed in this commit; public docs and skill guidance now describe existing checkout behavior for --source codex and unsupported_source.
security_implications: Public guidance avoids implying ingestion of Claude Code transcripts or non-public source data.
migration_notes: Docs now tell users to refresh indexes before cxs-v7-source-identity for source-aware identity and coverage.
